kpis.name: Retail — totals
kpis.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS total_nsv, SUM(units) AS total_units, ROUND(SUM(volume), 1) AS total_volume, ROUND(AVG(nsv), 2) AS avg_nsv FROM sales

trend.name: Retail — monthly trend
trend.sql: SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv, SUM(units) AS units, ROUND(SUM(volume), 1) AS volume FROM sales GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

region.name: Retail — NSV by region
region.sql: SELECT region, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv FROM sales GROUP BY region ORDER BY nsv DESC

pgroup.name: Retail — by product group
pgroup.sql: SELECT product_group, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv, SUM(units) AS units FROM sales GROUP BY product_group ORDER BY nsv DESC

heatmap.name: Retail — region × month
heatmap.sql: SELECT region, month, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv FROM sales GROUP BY region, month ORDER BY region, month

avb.name: Retail — actual vs budget (monthly)
avb.sql: SELECT a.month AS month, a.actual AS actual, b.budget AS budget FROM (SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS actual FROM sales GROUP BY month) a JOIN (SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(budget_nsv), 2) AS budget FROM budget GROUP BY month) b ON a.month = b.month ORDER BY a.month

variance.name: Retail — budget variance by region
variance.sql: SELECT a.region AS region, a.actual AS actual, b.budget AS budget, ROUND(a.actual - b.budget, 2) AS variance, ROUND(100.0 * (a.actual - b.budget) / NULLIF(b.budget, 0), 1) AS variance_pct FROM (SELECT region, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS actual FROM sales GROUP BY region) a JOIN (SELECT region, ROUND(SUM(budget_nsv), 2) AS budget FROM budget GROUP BY region) b ON a.region = b.region ORDER BY variance DESC

drill.name: Retail — region drilldown ({{region}})
drill.sql: SELECT month, product_group, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv FROM sales WHERE ({{region}} IS NULL OR region = {{region}}) GROUP BY month, product_group ORDER BY month, nsv DESC

prod_for_region.name: Retail — products in region ({{region}})
prod_for_region.sql: SELECT product_group, ROUND(SUM(nsv), 2) AS nsv, SUM(units) AS units FROM sales WHERE ({{region}} IS NULL OR region = {{region}}) GROUP BY product_group ORDER BY nsv DESC

region_opt.name: Retail — region options
region_opt.sql: SELECT DISTINCT region AS value, region AS label FROM sales ORDER BY 1

saas_kpis.name: SaaS — headline metrics
saas_kpis.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS mrr, ROUND(SUM(amount) * 12, 2) AS arr, COUNT(DISTINCT account_id) AS active_accounts, ROUND(SUM(amount) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT account_id), 0), 2) AS arpa FROM saas_invoices WHERE month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM saas_invoices)

saas_mrr_trend.name: SaaS — MRR trend
saas_mrr_trend.sql: SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS mrr, COUNT(DISTINCT account_id) AS active_accounts FROM saas_invoices GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

saas_mrr_by_plan.name: SaaS — MRR by plan over time
saas_mrr_by_plan.sql: SELECT month, plan, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS mrr FROM saas_invoices GROUP BY month, plan ORDER BY month, plan

saas_plan_mix.name: SaaS — plan mix (current month)
saas_plan_mix.sql: SELECT plan, COUNT(DISTINCT account_id) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS mrr FROM saas_invoices WHERE month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM saas_invoices) GROUP BY plan ORDER BY mrr DESC

saas_churn.name: SaaS — new vs churned by month
saas_churn.sql: SELECT month, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'new' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_accounts, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'churn' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS churned, ROUND(SUM(mrr_delta), 2) AS net_mrr_change FROM saas_subscription_events GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

saas_cohort.name: SaaS — cohort retention
saas_cohort.sql: SELECT a.signup_month AS cohort, (CAST(substr(i.month, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) * 12 + CAST(substr(i.month, 6, 2) AS INTEGER)) - (CAST(substr(a.signup_month, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) * 12 + CAST(substr(a.signup_month, 6, 2) AS INTEGER)) AS months_since, COUNT(DISTINCT i.account_id) AS active FROM saas_invoices i JOIN saas_accounts a ON i.account_id = a.account_id GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

saas_segment.name: SaaS — MRR by segment (current month)
saas_segment.sql: SELECT a.segment, ROUND(SUM(i.amount), 2) AS mrr, COUNT(DISTINCT i.account_id) AS accounts FROM saas_invoices i JOIN saas_accounts a ON i.account_id = a.account_id WHERE i.month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM saas_invoices) GROUP BY a.segment ORDER BY mrr DESC

saas_top_accounts.name: SaaS — top accounts by MRR
saas_top_accounts.sql: SELECT a.account, a.segment, i.plan, ROUND(SUM(i.amount), 2) AS mrr FROM saas_invoices i JOIN saas_accounts a ON i.account_id = a.account_id WHERE i.month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM saas_invoices) GROUP BY a.account, a.segment, i.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC LIMIT 15

saas_mrr_filt.name: SaaS — MRR trend (filtered)
saas_mrr_filt.sql: SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS mrr FROM saas_invoices WHERE ({{plan}} IS NULL OR plan = {{plan}}) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

saas_plan_opt.name: SaaS — plan options
saas_plan_opt.sql: SELECT plan AS value, plan AS label FROM saas_plans ORDER BY tier

web_kpis.name: Web — headline metrics
web_kpis.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(pageviews) AS total_pageviews, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(converted) / COUNT(*), 2) AS conversion_pct, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(bounced) / COUNT(*), 1) AS bounce_pct FROM web_sessions

web_traffic_trend.name: Web — sessions & conversions by month
web_traffic_trend.sql: SELECT month, COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(converted) AS conversions FROM web_sessions GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

web_source.name: Web — sessions by source
web_source.sql: SELECT utm_source, COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(converted) AS conversions, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(converted) / COUNT(*), 2) AS conv_rate_pct FROM web_sessions GROUP BY utm_source ORDER BY sessions DESC

web_device.name: Web — sessions by device
web_device.sql: SELECT device, COUNT(*) AS sessions FROM web_sessions GROUP BY device ORDER BY sessions DESC

web_country.name: Web — sessions by country
web_country.sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(converted) AS conversions FROM web_sessions GROUP BY country ORDER BY sessions DESC LIMIT 10

web_funnel.name: Web — signup funnel
web_funnel.sql: SELECT step, MIN(page) AS page, COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) AS sessions FROM web_pageviews WHERE step IS NOT NULL GROUP BY step ORDER BY step

web_top_pages.name: Web — top pages
web_top_pages.sql: SELECT page, COUNT(*) AS pageviews FROM web_pageviews GROUP BY page ORDER BY pageviews DESC LIMIT 10

web_filt_trend.name: Web — monthly sessions (filtered)
web_filt_trend.sql: SELECT month, COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(converted) AS conversions FROM web_sessions WHERE ({{source}} IS NULL OR utm_source = {{source}}) AND ({{device}} IS NULL OR device = {{device}}) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

web_source_opt.name: Web — source options
web_source_opt.sql: SELECT DISTINCT utm_source AS value, utm_source AS label FROM web_sessions ORDER BY 1

web_device_opt.name: Web — device options
web_device_opt.sql: SELECT DISTINCT device AS value, device AS label FROM web_sessions ORDER BY 1

fin_kpis.name: Finance — headline metrics
fin_kpis.sql: SELECT (SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) FROM fin_invoices) AS total_billed, (SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) FROM fin_payments) AS total_collected, (SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) FROM fin_invoices WHERE status <> 'paid') AS open_ar, (SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) FROM fin_expenses WHERE month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM fin_expenses)) AS latest_month_expenses

fin_cashflow.name: Finance — monthly cashflow
fin_cashflow.sql: SELECT m.month AS month, COALESCE(b.billed, 0) AS billed, COALESCE(c.collected, 0) AS collected, COALESCE(e.expenses, 0) AS expenses, ROUND(COALESCE(c.collected, 0) - COALESCE(e.expenses, 0), 2) AS net_cash FROM (SELECT DISTINCT month FROM fin_expenses) m LEFT JOIN (SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS billed FROM fin_invoices GROUP BY month) b ON m.month = b.month LEFT JOIN (SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS collected FROM fin_payments GROUP BY month) c ON m.month = c.month LEFT JOIN (SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS expenses FROM fin_expenses GROUP BY month) e ON m.month = e.month ORDER BY m.month

fin_ar_aging.name: Finance — AR aging (open invoices)
fin_ar_aging.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN days_past <= 0 THEN 'Current' WHEN days_past <= 30 THEN '1-30 days' WHEN days_past <= 60 THEN '31-60 days' WHEN days_past <= 90 THEN '61-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END AS bucket, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS amount, COUNT(*) AS invoices FROM (SELECT amount, date_diff('day', due_date, DATE '2026-05-31') AS days_past FROM fin_invoices WHERE status <> 'paid') t GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY MIN(days_past)

fin_expense_dept.name: Finance — expenses by department
fin_expense_dept.sql: SELECT department, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS amount FROM fin_expenses GROUP BY department ORDER BY amount DESC

fin_expense_cat_trend.name: Finance — expenses by category over time
fin_expense_cat_trend.sql: SELECT month, category, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS amount FROM fin_expenses GROUP BY month, category ORDER BY month, amount DESC

fin_headcount_trend.name: Finance — total headcount by month
fin_headcount_trend.sql: SELECT month, SUM(headcount) AS headcount FROM fin_headcount GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

fin_expense_filt.name: Finance — monthly expenses (filtered)
fin_expense_filt.sql: SELECT month, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS amount FROM fin_expenses WHERE ({{department}} IS NULL OR department = {{department}}) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

fin_dept_opt.name: Finance — department options
fin_dept_opt.sql: SELECT DISTINCT department AS value, department AS label FROM fin_expenses ORDER BY 1
